title: Prenatal Medicine Has Been Staring at Half the Biology for a Century
description: A landmark Lancet review finds that a father's nutrition, stress, smoking, and environmental exposures before conception measurably shape pregnancy outcomes and the child's long-term health — through epigenetic changes in sperm and chemical signals in semen that science is only now beginning to map. Prenatal care has been built almost entirely around the maternal body. The evidence says that was always incomplete. Here's what the biology actually shows, and what it means for medicine.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Prenatal Medicine Has Been Staring at Half the Biology for a Century
